summary: Tested Social Media (Facebook/Instagram) and WhatsApp integration for iteration 68. All 12 backend API tests pass (100%). Fixed a bug in /api/whatsapp/send endpoint where language_code defaulted to 'en' instead of auto-detecting from template.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: /api/whatsapp/send endpoint now auto-detects language_code from template (was defaulting to 'en')
Fixed: /api/whatsapp/send-to-lead/{lead_id} endpoint now auto-detects language_code from template
Facebook posting works with Page Access Token derived from System User token (get_page_access_token function)
WhatsApp template sync correctly stores language_code field (hello_world=en_US, promote_asr_enterprises=en)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All social media and WhatsApp integrations verified working. Facebook posts successfully created (IDs: 963923893475549_122117544789241422, 963923893475549_122117544747241422). WhatsApp templates synced with correct language codes. Auto-detect language feature now works on all WhatsApp send endpoints.

features_tested.POST /api/social/test-connection: PASS - Facebook and Instagram both connected
features_tested.GET /api/social/settings: PASS - Returns correct Page ID and Account ID
features_tested.POST /api/social/posts/create: PASS - Facebook text post created successfully
features_tested.GET /api/whatsapp/templates: PASS - Returns templates with language_code field
features_tested.POST /api/whatsapp/settings/test: PASS - API connection successful, quality GREEN

bug_fixed.issue: language_code defaulted to 'en' instead of None, bypassing auto-detection from template
bug_fixed.fix: Changed data.get('language_code', 'en') to data.get('language_code') to allow None value for auto-detection
bug_fixed.verification: Test now passes - hello_world template sends with en_US language code

rca_of_issue: The /api/whatsapp/send endpoint was hardcoding language_code='en' as default, which caused template send failures when the actual template language was different (e.g., en_US). The send_whatsapp_template function has auto-detection logic that looks up the template's language_code from the database, but this was being bypassed because a non-None value was always passed.
